metadata.title: Discrete Frequencies in DFT Quiz
metadata.author: Axono
metadata.description: A quiz about discrete frequencies in DFT.

question.text: What are the discrete frequency bins for a sequence with length N=250 and sample interval Δt=0.02s?
question.explanation: fk = k / (N * delta_t) = k / (250 * 0.02) = k / 5 = 0.2k Hz. The sampling frequency fs = 1/delta_t = 50 Hz, and fk = k * fs / N = k * 50 / 250 = 0.2k Hz.

question.text: For a DFT of length N, what is the relationship between the fundamental frequency f1 and the sample rate fs?
question.explanation: The fundamental frequency f1 = 1 / (N * delta_t) = fs / N. This is the frequency whose period equals exactly the full DFT window length.
